decision.scope: K-12 family-communication tool; approved only when student and parent contact PII flows through the district's tokenization vault.

rubric.description: Vendor processes student records in a FERPA-compliant manner.
Vendor obtains verifiable parental consent for users under 13.
Student/parent contact PII is tokenized before persistence in vendor systems.
rubric.notes: Approved subject to data_vault_targets[0] — vendor never sees raw email or phone.

conditions.description: Student and parent contact PII (email, phone) MUST be tokenized through the district's vault before flowing to FamilyReach. Vendor receives tokens only.
conditions.violation_response: Approval suspended pending review; district disables the tenant.

conditions.description: Detokenization may only be performed by Springfield USD principals and compliance-officers acting on documented family-services requests. All reveal events are appended to the district audit stream.

rationale: FamilyReach 2.4's product is a fit for the district's family-communication goal, but the Tool Card discloses storage of raw student/parent email and phone for 90 days. The district approves use only when contact PII never leaves the district perimeter in raw form — tokenized at ingest via the district's Skyflow vault, with reveal limited to two roles operating under documented family-services review. Vendor signs a contractual confirmation that tokens are sufficient for product functionality.

data_vault_targets.notes: US-East residency. Key rotation: 90-day. Reveal requests outside the two named roles are denied at the broker layer and logged as policy violations.

history.note: Technical vaulting condition added per FERPA review.
